analysis.summary: The technician performed a network stack performance test on a Windows machine using iperf3, then connected via SSH to a Linux server to inspect Docker Nextcloud AIO volumes, check disk space, and verify network connectivity with a ping test. The technician also briefly checked the display resolution settings on the Windows machine.
actions_taken: Initiated and reviewed iperf3 server results for a network stack performance test on the Windows machine (client and server on 10.100.7.173).
Navigated browser to files.budman.pw, potentially to verify or download iperf3 binaries.
Connected to a Linux server via SSH using PuTTY.
Inspected Docker Nextcloud AIO volumes on the Linux server, noting an error for a specific volume name.
Checked disk space utilization on the Linux server using `df -h`.
Performed a successful ping test from the Linux server to IP address 10.7.3.250.
Accessed Windows Display settings to observe the current screen resolution (1920x868).
issues_observed: iperf3 bandwidth varied significantly (1.15 to 3.07 Gbits/sec) during the network stack performance test.
Error 'No such volume' when inspecting a Docker volume with a specific name (`nextcloud_aio_*`).
Unusual or incomplete `df -h` output on the Linux server showing very small mount points for `/dev/root`, `/dev/sda15`, and `/dev/sdb1`.
Windows display resolution was set to 1920x868 instead of the recommended 1920x1080.
machines_touched: Windows client machine (IP 10.100.7.173 observed in iperf3 logs)
notable_events: The iperf3 test was conducted as a loopback test on the same machine (client and server on 10.100.7.173), measuring the local network stack performance.
An error was encountered during Docker volume inspection, indicating a potential misconfiguration or typo in the volume name.
The `df -h` command showed a potentially unusual disk partitioning setup on the Linux server.
The technician observed the non-native display resolution on the Windows machine but did not change it within the recording.
concerns: The 'No such volume' error in Docker was not explicitly resolved or investigated further within the recording, potentially leaving a configuration issue unresolved.
The non-native display resolution was observed but not corrected, which might impact user experience or display quality.

tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician moved methodically between different systems and diagnostic tools (iperf3, Docker, df -h, ping, display settings). The workflow was logical, addressing various potential areas of concern. However, some observed issues (Docker error, display resolution) were not fully resolved within the captured segment.
coaching_flags: Follow up on observed Docker 'No such volume' errors to ensure complete resolution of configuration issues.
Address identified non-native display resolution settings to improve user experience, if applicable to the issue being troubleshooted.
